function.name: _app_save_define_object
function.description:
Save or update a single object structure (Define Object) in the application definition.

【Purpose】
- AI modifies object structure
- Create new Define objects
- Automatically fix configuration errors
- Application structure adjustment

【Prerequisites (Must Follow)】
- Must first call _app_get_define_object_type to get valid type
- Must call _app_get_define_object to read current structure before modification
- Cannot construct structure out of thin air

【Important Notes】
- This operation will modify the draft version structure
- Will not directly affect the published version
- Should call verification interface before publishing after saving

【Hierarchy Rules】

1️⃣ App-level objects:
- scope must be "App"

2️⃣ Module-level objects:
- scope must be the id of the corresponding ModuleDefine
- Cannot be empty

【Mandatory Rules】
- scope, id, type, content are all required
- type must come from _app_get_define_object_type
- content must be a complete JSON string
- If scope and type hierarchy do not match, must report error
- It is recommended to call _app_check_setting for verification after saving

scope.description:
Parent scope identifier.

- App-level objects: fill in "App"
- Module-level objects: fill in the id of the corresponding ModuleDefine

id.description: Object unique identifier id. If it is a new object, it must be a valid new id; if it is an update, it must be an existing id

type.description: Object type. Must come from the return value of _app_get_define_object_type

content.description: Complete object structure JSON string. Must be a complete Define object structure, partial fields are not allowed
